description: Small sandwich cookies made from almond flour, egg whites, and sugar, with a smooth shell, delicate interior, and a filling such as ganache, buttercream, or jam.
preparation_steps: Whip egg whites with sugar to make a stable meringue.
Fold in almond flour and powdered sugar to form the batter.
Pipe small rounds and let them rest before baking.
Bake until the shells are set and form feet.
Cool and sandwich with the chosen filling.

health_advice.benefits: Small portions can make them easier to enjoy in moderation compared with larger desserts.
health_advice.risks: They are still sugar-rich and fillings can add extra fat and calories.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Treat them as an occasional dessert rather than an everyday snack.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional French macarons are usually gluten-free because they use almond flour, but fillings and cross-contact can change this.
